text.title: File Search and Vector Store Rules
text.summary: Rules for creating vector stores, uploading files, verifying indexing completion, and handling citations in File Search responses.

rule.statement: Poll file.status == "completed" before querying after upload. File processing is asynchronous and may take seconds to minutes for large PDFs.
rule.rationale: Querying immediately after files.create returns results that do not include the newly uploaded file. The file is not searchable until processing completes.

rule.statement: Maintain a file_id to filename mapping in your application. File Search citations reference file_id, not file name.
rule.rationale: The API does not return file names in annotations — only file_ids. Without a mapping, citations cannot be displayed to users with meaningful source labels.

rule.statement: Delete vector stores and their associated files when a session ends.
rule.rationale: Vector stores incur storage billing independently of requests. Session-scoped stores created for one-off queries will accumulate cost if not deleted.

rule.statement: Set max_prompt_tokens and max_completion_tokens on runs in multi-user deployments to cap per-run cost.
rule.rationale: Without per-run token caps, a single large document query in a shared deployment can consume disproportionate tokens and increase cost unpredictably.

example.note: Context manager guarantees cleanup even if an exception occurs during the session.

antipattern.description: Querying a File Search-enabled assistant immediately after uploading a file.
antipattern.reason: File processing is asynchronous. Querying before file.status == "completed" returns results that do not include the uploaded content, producing silently incomplete answers.
